GateDocument.TextWithNodes: "Where to eat, sleep and stay in Launceston, Tasmania", "The northern parts of Tasmania rarely get the same air time as the southernmost state's pretty capital, but it has plenty of beauty to offer. Launceston is packed with history, scenic drives and picturesque hikes. Quaint English architecture merges with sprawling farmlands where you can pick your own berries or take a photo amongst awe-inspiring lavender fields. So pack a friend and a car, and plan a food (and wine)-laden adventure. SEE. Bridestowe Estate. The product of a London perfumer, a packet of French lavender seeds and the cool Tasmanian climate, Bridestowe Estate has mastered Provence-worthy lavender sustainable production since 1921. It's a picturesque drive just 40 minutes north of Launceston, and the world's largest privately owned lavender farm hosts something in the ballpark of 50 000 visitors each year. With over 200 kilometres of winding rows, come for the Jacquemus-esque photo opp and stay for a masterclass in aromatherapy. During the peak flowering period from mid-December to mid-January, the estate dries and distills flowers into the purest oil (Tasmanian lavender oil is largely regarded as amongst the finest in the world) and spa products infused with the calming fragrance. Or indulge in a different kind of self-care, with sweet yet herbaceous lavender ice-cream or shortbread. The lavender oil distilling happens right there on site, so Bridestowe emanates the soft scent of one of nature's most beloved fragrant notes, no matter where amongst the rolling purple hills you're posed for your picture. Build your trip to the Apple Isle around the harvest dates. Cataract Gorge. An urban retreat not far from the Launceston CBD, unwind amongst local wildlife and panoramic scenes at Cataract Gorge. Complete with lookouts, a footbridge and pool, bring only the necessities–swimmers, spf and a sense of adventure. First built in 1972, the First Basin Chairlift is a great, albeit retro way to enjoy a view of the whole gorge. After a day on the trails, spare time to relax and rehydrate with the Gorge Restaurant's extensive list of Tasmanian wines. STAY. Quamby Home. This beautifully-appointed 1870s-built home sits amongst 300-plus acres of farmland in the Meander Valley, 20 minutes outside of Launceston. The perfect place to take in the world's cleanest air and explore the southern isle without sacrificing any sense of luxury, it comfortably sleeps ten, so makes for an excellent group gathering. You can enjoy toasting marshmallows in the open fires both inside or out, no matter the season, or gazing at the abundance of stars that become visible when you're this far outside of a city. The kitchen comes fully-equipped for chef-quality meals and the generous bathrooms boast amenities by Grown Alchemist. Don't be surprised if a visit here doesn' make you want to go home and redecorate. Quamby Home's chic interior perfectly demonstrates how the practical and the luxurious don't have to be mutually exclusive terms. EAT. Stillwater. Once a waterfront flour mill, Stillwater's rustic and refined interiors make a statement of its 1830s architecture and exposed beams. Settle in for a relaxed long lunch, indulgent tasting menu or late-night drinks at the wine bar to experience the crop of Tasmania's distinctive seasons. Serving the best of the state's lush paddocks and abundant southern oceans, consider Stillwater's modern-Australian menu a 90 minute food tour of Tasmania. DRINK. Clover Hill. Rivaling the iconic champagne of France, Clover Hill is renowned for its commitment to MГ©thode Traditionnelle, crafting quality sparkling wines unique to Tasmania. Spend an afternoon at the Cellar Door, sampling the estate's award-winning vintages, whilst overlooking 22 hectares of vines and the Bass Strait."
